description:
Rewrite runtime/src/heap.rs Heap struct to §6.1 shape:

  struct Heap {
      block_start: *mut u8,
      bump_top:    *mut u8,
      block_end:   *mut u8,
      size_class:  u8,
      low_live_streak: u8,
      pressure: AtomicBool,
      pub schemas: Rc<RefCell<SchemaRegistry>>,
      pub gc_run_count: u64,
      alloc_count: u64,  // since-last-gc; backs live_count()
  }

- alloc(size) is bump only: bump_top.add(size); if > block_end -> note_alloc_pressure (set flag) AND allocate a fresh larger block, abandoning the old (memory-leak grow). Real Cheney lands in fz-siu.8.
- note_alloc_pressure also fires when bump_top crosses gc_threshold_bytes (kept for back-compat with .19.6 per-process threshold setter; final removal in .8/.9).
- Drop: free block_start (and any abandoned blocks tracked in a side Vec).
- gc(&mut self) -> stub; just increments gc_run_count.
- live_count() -> alloc_count (since last gc). freelist_len() -> 0 (no freelist).
- bytes_used() = bump_top.offset_from(block_start).
- Initial size: SIZE_TABLE[0]=1 KiB per §6.3 wired (the smaller initial enforces grow-path coverage; growth via bump-overflow).
- alloc_* (struct/list_cons/map/bitstring/closure/float/vec_*) unchanged in interface, just go through new alloc().

Acceptance: build green; all non-mark-sweep tests pass; growth path covered by an existing fixture (e.g. list-heavy test runs without OOM).
title: fz-cps.GC.1.2: Heap struct = bump-only per §6.1
